title: The Mideast Is Baffled by Trump’s Call to Expand Abraham Accords
summary: The president said more countries should be required to recognize Israel as part of a deal to end the war with Iran. Analysts say the chances of that happening are close to zero.

title: ‘Both Parties Kind of Get It Wrong’: The Young Men Who May Swing the Midterms
summary: Many Gen Z men who voted for Donald Trump are dismayed by his time in office. But they say they are not hearing an appealing pitch from Democrats, either.

title: James Talarico Opens Texas Senate Campaign Against Ken Paxton With Houston Rally
summary: Personal attacks and a packed campaign rally set the tone for what is likely to be a rancorous contest in Texas for U.S. Senate between James Talarico and Ken Paxton.

title: Judge Declines, for Now, to Block Mail-In Voting Changes Ordered by Trump
summary: The ruling allowed the Trump administration to continue to pursue changes that include enlisting the Postal Service in checking voters against a national database.

title: Michigan Governor Gretchen Whitmer Will Not Run for President in 2028
summary: Governor Whitmer of Michigan had been viewed as part of a field of possible Democratic candidates. She has been governor for two terms, and is barred from running again.

title: In Argentina, U.S. Tech Billionaire Peter Thiel Finds An Escape
summary: The billionaire’s new roots in Argentina are said to be partly motivated by concerns about the future of the United States and shared beliefs with Argentina’s right-wing leader.

title: I Tried to Sell My House With A.I.
summary: Over five frantic days, I gambled my family’s life savings on a hunch that A.I. could outperform a real estate agent.

title: Watchdog Agency Seeks to Reverse Its Win Against Firm Run by Winklevoss Twins
summary: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission filed jointly with a company run by the Winklevoss twins to overturn their settlement.

title: Dell Gets a $9.7 Billion Defense Contract. Trump’s Portfolio Stands to Benefit.
summary: The president’s family says his stock purchases are automatically timed, but recent acquisitions of the computer company’s shares raise questions.

title: Bari Weiss Names Nick Bilton to Lead CBS’s ’60 Minutes’ in Major Shake-Up
summary: Bari Weiss, CBS’s editor in chief, named Nick Bilton, a tech journalist and filmmaker, as the show’s executive producer. The network also fired two on-air correspondents.

title: Trump Refiles $10 Billion Lawsuit Against The Wall Street Journal
summary: An earlier version of the defamation suit, which focused on an article about a birthday note to Jeffrey Epstein, was dismissed by a judge.

title: Two Papers Named ‘Star’ Will Duel in D.C. to Win Readers From The Washington Post
summary: First, The Star was born. Now, The Washington Star is reborn. Both are hoping to win readers ceded by recent layoffs at The Washington Post.

title: Anthropic Tops OpenAI to Become the World’s Most Valuable A.I. Start-Up
summary: Anthropic raised $65 billion in new fund-raising that put its value at $900 billion, ahead of OpenAI’s last valuation of $730 billion, as the companies duel for A.I. dominance.

title: Memphis Residents Sue Federal Agents Over Arrest Tactics Amid Crime and Immigration Crackdown
summary: A federal task force in the city has been targeting crime and undocumented immigrants since October. Observers say they have faced retaliation for trying to track the agents’ work.

title: What’s Inside New York’s $269 Billion Budget: Second-Home Tax, Restrictions on ICE and More
summary: Gov. Kathy Hochul used the state budget as a vehicle for policy initiatives, including a new second-home tax and a push to make the state less hospitable to immigration agents.

title: Mamdani Vowed to Back Espaillat. Now He May Endorse a D.S.A. Challenger.
summary: The mayor is seriously considering getting behind Darializa Avila Chevalier, a democratic socialist challenging Representative Adriano Espaillat in an Upper Manhattan House primary.

title: Paul McCartney on His New Album and the Beatles’ Earliest Days
summary: On his new album, the ex-Beatle revisits his past and revels in simply continuing to create.

title: Greenpeace’s Long War With a Pipeline Titan Enters a Strange New Phase
summary: This month, a North Dakota court barred Greenpeace from saying what it wanted in a European court, an unusual move. The environmental group says it is forging ahead.

title: Happy, Bronx Zoo Elephant at Center of Animal Rights Case, Is Dead at 55
summary: Activists had sued in a bid to secure her the fundamental human right to bodily liberty. Zoo officials said she was well cared for and called the lawsuit frivolous.

title: Lizzo Addresses the Backlash and Performs Live at The New York Times
summary: During a special edition of “Popcast,” the singer discussed her new album and the public fallout from a series of lawsuits. Then she performed a stripped-down set.

title: Three Ways Trump Is Losing the War in Iran
summary: Three reasons Trump is losing the war in Iran.

title: Our Military Is Built for the Wrong Century
summary: Ukraine and Iran have shown us that war as we’ve known it is over.

title: Quarantine Unit for Americans Exposed to Ebola to Open in Kenya Friday
summary: But U.S. officials are still working to find centers in Europe to accept Americans who need advanced treatment, seen as critical to improving odds of survival.

title: Everlane Co-Founder, Shocked by Shein Acquisition, Wants a Redo
summary: Michael Preysman, who ran the brand for a decade, can’t believe what happened to the company he built. Can he start over?

title: Man Sentenced to 40 Years in Murders of 4 Sleeping Homeless Men
summary: The judge who sentenced Randy Rodriguez Santos said his case exemplified the “coming together of three horrible symptoms of this city: homelessness, mental illness and narcotics abuse.”

title: Supreme Court Says Death Row Inmate Can Challenge Exclusion of Black Jurors
summary: Terry Pitchford was convicted in 2006 for his role in the murder of a shopkeeper by a 12-member jury that included one Black member.
